item.title: Agamir Somoy formally launched
item.description: Information minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Tuesday said that a government should move away from controlling the media and instead act as a partner in promoting media freedom....Educational Resources

item.description: The Praava Health observed World Family Doctor Day 2026 under the theme compassionate care in a digital world through a rally, roundtable discussion, cake cutting and recognition ceremony, honouring the contributions of family medicine doctors in......

item.title: DU holds seminar on higher education opportunities in France
item.description: The International Centre for Ocean Governance of Dhaka University organised a seminar titled Higher Education Opportunities in France at the Professor MA Latif Auditorium of DU geology department on Tuesday. ...

item.title: Bangladesh lags behind in adopting plan: experts
item.description: Bangladesh is falling behind its regional competitors in adopting a coherent responsible business conduct framework, mainly due to two major instruments, mandatory human rights and environmental due diligence law and a national action plan on RBC......Maps

item.title: Manual challans to become obsolete on July 1
item.description: The Finance Division on Tuesday decided not to accept any payments through manual challans from July 1 in a bid to reduce high interest payments on government borrowing....

item.title: Disqualified candidates for prison guards block Dhaka-Mawa highway
item.description: Disqualified candidates who vied for the jobs of male and female prison guards blocked the Dhaka-Mawa highway on Tuesday morning, alleging that the Department of Prisons disqualified them intentionally....

item.title: Govt plans more jute mills’ privatisation
item.description: Commerce, industries, textiles and jute minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir on Tuesday said that the government aimed to transform Bangladesh’s traditional jute sector into a $5 billion to $7 billion export-oriented industry through stronger private sector participation, modernisation and product diversification......

item.title: BB warns banks against dollar market manipulation
item.description: Bangladesh Bank governor Mostaqur Rahman on Tuesday warned banks against manipulating the dollar market and said strict action would be taken against banks found involved in irregular practices......Expat Community Forum

item.title: Robi’s hiring of foreign executive challenged
item.description: The appointment of Shoaib Baig as chief human resources officer of Robi Axiata has raised serious questions over the telecom operator’s recruitment practices as the company ignored allegations of ethical misconduct, irregular appointments and controversial dismissals linked to his previous employers in Pakistan......

item.title: Bangladesh, EU discuss trade, non-tariff barriers
item.description: Bangladesh and the European Union discussed bilateral trade relations, investment opportunities, and the removal of existing non-tariff barriers......

item.title: Padma turns into vast sandbars
item.description: The Padma River, once the lifeline of northwestern Bangladesh, is increasingly turning into a vast expanse of sandbars during the dry season, posing fears of ecological degradation, desertification, and growing economic hardship throughout the river basin....

item.title: BB tightens card-to-MFS transfer rules
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has imposed stricter conditions on transferring money from bank cards to mobile financial services accounts, commonly known as ‘add money’, amid rising concerns over cyber fraud and unauthorised digital transactions......

item.title: No manual tendering from July 1
item.description: To ensure full implementation of electronic Government Procurement (e-GP) in line with the Public Procurement Act, there will be no further scope for conducting manual tenders from 1 July......

item.title: Mass media policy, commission to be formed by Aug: info minister
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Tuesday said that a modern and time-appropriate mass media policy, along with a Media Commission, would be formulated by August to protect the interests of journalists, media owners and newspaper employees....Press Release Distribution

item.title: Iran warns US against new attacks
item.description: Iran’s army warned on Tuesday it would ‘open new fronts’ against the United States if it resumes attacks, after president Donald Trump said he had held off launching a planned new offensive in the hope of striking a deal....

item.title: Steel millers oppose power tariff hike
item.description: Claiming that Bangladesh’s steel sector is now in the intensive care unit (ICU)), industry leaders on Tuesday strongly demanded that the government refrain from raising electricity tariffs further to prevent the widespread shutdown of manufacturing plants......

item.title: US extends sanctions waiver on Russian at-sea oil
item.description: The US Treasury on Monday extended by 30 days its sanctions waiver for Russian oil cargoes already at sea, as global energy prices continue to surge due to the Iran war......

item.title: Pvt hospitals asked to admit measles patients
item.description: The Directorate General of Health Services on Tuesday directed all private hospitals and healthcare facilities across the country to compulsorily admit and treat measles and suspected measles patients....

item.title: Fakhrul hails ILO support for work safety, labour welfare
item.description: The Local Government, Rural Development and Cooperatives Minister, Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir, on Tuesday said International Labour Organisation (ILO) can contribute immensely in ensuring safe working environment and labour welfare in Bangladesh......

item.description: Global smartphone brand vivo Bangladesh is showcasing inspiring stories of hope, ambition and determination through its ‘Capture the Future’ initiative in collaboration with SOS Children’s Villages Bangladesh......Expat Community Forum

item.description: The government on Tuesday presented its achievements during the first three months in office while opposition parties, in and outside the parliament, challenged the claims....

item.description: Mercantile Bank Training Institute recently organised a comprehensive workshop on ‘Awareness and Capacity Building for Risk-Based Supervision Implementation’ with an aim of strengthening participants’ understanding of effective risk management and supervisory practices in the banking sector......

item.description: Bank Asia PLC on Monday partnered with Unimart Limited to offer exciting cashback privileges for its credit cardholders, said a press release......

item.description: SBAC Bank PLC signed an agreement on Monday to participate in the Bangladesh Bank Refinance Fund of Tk 3,000 crore to the Cluster Financing Scheme for the CMSME sector......

item.description: Samsung Bangladesh on Tuesday launched a campaign on the occasion of Eid-ul-Azha across all authorised outlets nationwide......Expat Community Forum

item.description: A delegation from the Institute of Cost and Management Accountants of Bangladesh paid a courtesy call on finance minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury on Monday at his office in the capital......

item.description: MTB Foundation, as part of its project titled ‘Climbing for Climate, Building for Communities — A Climate Resilience Initiative of MTB Foundation’ recently organised a flag-off ceremony for a pioneering Bangladeshi mountaineer Yasmin Lisa, as she embarks on her high-altitude Himalayan expedition......

item.title: Akij Agro launches modern fisheries project under JV
item.description: A modern fisheries project, launched as a joint venture between Akij Agro and Livestock Limited and Anhui New Ready Co Limited, was inaugurated in Kishoreganj on Saturday, said a press release......

item.title: Meghna Bank, BB sign deals under CMSME refinancing schemes
item.description: Meghna Bank PLC has recently signed participation agreements with the Bangladesh Bank under cluster financing scheme and the FSFDCMSME Refinancing Scheme through BB’s SME and Special Programs Department to strengthen financing support for cottage, micro, small and medium enterprises......

item.title: LPG consumers suffer as oversight remains poor
item.description: THE increase in prices of liquefied petroleum gas has doubly jeopardised households and small businesses as consumers do not only need to pay more for the gas but, in many cases, also need to spend way too much in the event of weak enforcement of the prices on the retail market. The Energy Regulatory Commission increased the price of a 12-kilogram cylinder of gas......

item.title: Govt must prioritise growth and job creation
item.description: ABOUT 1.4 million people freshly sliding into poverty in 2025, as a World Bank report says, is worrying. The World Bank’s report, presented at a seminar that the Policy Research Institute and the World Bank organised in Dhaka on May 18, says that the poverty rate reached 21.4 per cent, which was 20.5 per cent in 2024. The near-term outlook has also weakened, with the real......

item.title: Unfinished journey of tea workers
item.description: ONE hundred and five years ago this day in 1921, thousands of tea workers from gardens in Sylhet and Chittagong walked out onto roads of the colonial Bengal with a single desire — to go home. They carried no weapons and harboured no rage. They only longed for a return to their native land, their ‘muluk.’ The journey, brutally crushed at the Chandpur steamer......Maps

item.title: Health depends on cross-ministry action
item.description: EFFECTIVE coordination between ministries plays a crucial role in improving public health in Bangladesh. Health outcomes are shaped not only by hospitals, doctors, or medicines, but by a wide range of social and economic conditions. The World Health Organization’s Social Determinants of Health framework highlights that income,education, nutrition, housing......

item.title: Untapped economic opportunity
item.description: EVERY year, Eid-ul-Adha creates one of the largest seasonal economic activities in Bangladesh. Millions of people become involved in cattle farming, transportation, livestock trading, butchering, hide collection, sanitation work, and small-scale commerce. Yet despite this enormous economic movement, much of its value disappears within days because Bangladesh still......Expat Community Forum

item.title: Art of teaching executives
item.description: TEACHING is undoubtedly an art, an art unlike any other. Art is the soulful weaving of expression and meaning, shaped by skill and illuminated by imagination. Teaching can be seen through the same lens. It is a creative act that blends knowledge, inspiration and human connection to ignite minds and transform lives. A painter does not repeat identical brushstrokes......

item.title: Cuba’s resistance
item.description: CUBA’S history is not merely that of an island nation; it is a striking story of resistance against colonialism, slavery, and imperialism. Few countries have endured more than six and a half decades of sustained hostility from the United States while maintaining a socialist system on the very doorstep of the US itself. Despite relentless American antagonism, Cuba has......

item.title: Guardiola swerves City exit talk
item.description: Pep Guardiola said he would speak to Manchester City's hierarchy over his future after a 1-1 draw at Bournemouth ended his team's hopes of winning the Premier League....

item.title: How Arsenal banished 'nearly men' tag to end 22-year title wait
item.description: Just four years ago, Arsenal were wallowing in recriminations and regret after finishing 24 points behind Premier League champions Manchester City....

item.title: Arsenal win Premier League after City held by Bournemouth
item.description: Thousands of Arsenal fans descended on the Emirates Stadium to celebrate a first Premier League title in 22 years on Tuesday after Manchester City were held 1-1 by Bournemouth. ...

item.title: Coast Guard seizes fuel, edible oil in Chattogram
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard seized a huge quantity of fuel oil and edible oil in two separate drives in Chattogram’s Patenga and EPZ areas on Monday night and Tuesday....

item.description: Transport workers in Naogaon on Tuesday went on an indefinite strike protesting an attack on fellow workers allegedly carried out by the leaders and activists of Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, the student wing of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party....

item.description: The Transparency International Bangladesh on Tuesday expressed concern over the draft National Human Rights Commission Act 2026, saying that several provisions risk turning the proposed commission into a government-dependent body and undermining its independence....

item.title: WHO fears rapid spread of deadly Ebola outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization on Tuesday voiced concern about the ‘scale and speed’ of an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, which has killed an estimated 131 people, and warned it could be lengthy....

item.title: France, Turkey lead Europe in jail overcrowding
item.description: France and Turkey have the highest levels of prison overcrowding among Council of Europe member states, with 131 inmates for every 100 places, a specially commissioned report said Tuesday....

item.title: Indian central govt takes control of key stretches in Siliguri Corridor
item.description: India’s central government will take charge of some of West Bengal’s most strategically important stretches of highway, many of them passing through the narrow Siliguri Corridor or Chicken’s Neck, reported The Telegraph Online on Monday....

item.title: ICC seeks arrest warrant for Israeli finance minister
item.description: Israel’s far-right finance minister Bezalel Smotrich said Tuesday that the International Criminal Court prosecutor has requested an arrest warrant against him, accusing the Palestinian Authority of pushing for the move....

item.title: Russian strikes kill 4 in north Ukraine
item.description: Moscow’s missile and drone attacks across northern Ukraine killed at least four people on Tuesday, Kyiv said, as president Volodymyr Zelensky has been warning of possible renewed Russian offensives from the north....

item.title: Indian mission official found dead in Ctg
item.description: The police recovered the body of an official of the Indian Assistant High Commission in Chattogram from the mission’s office in the city’s Khulshi area on Tuesday morning....

item.description: A school teacher was killed and another was injured in a road accident involving a head-on collision at Badalgachi upazila in Naogaon on Tuesday....

item.description: The Bangladesh Standards and Testing Institution, the national standards body of Bangladesh, will observe World Metrology Day today, May 20, said a press release....

item.title: JU forms probe body over alleged harassment of journalists
item.description: Jahangirnagar University authorities have formed a five-member committee to investigate allegations that journalists were obstructed, verbally abused and threatened while carrying out professional duties on campus, according to an official order issued by the administration....

item.title: 20 villages razed, 11 injured in nor’wester
item.description: At least 20 villages in five upazilas have been affected by the nor’wester that swept over Lalmonirhat Monday night....

item.title: Private tutor found dead in C’nawabganj
item.description: The police recovered the body of a private tutor stuffed inside a sack from a house in the Namo Shankarbati Chowkapara area under Chapainawabganj municipality on Tuesday morning....

item.description: The Chittagong Medical University organised a discussion, celebrating University Day, at the seminar room of the university on Monday....

item.description: Speakers at a policy dialogue in Rajshahi on Tuesday warned that excessive pesticide use and chemical-dependent farming were triggering a ‘silent pollination crisis’ in the country, threatening biodiversity and food security....

item.title: One missing after two shot in Padma char
item.description: A man went missing after he, along with another man, had been wounded in shots fired allegedly by robbers early Tuesday in the Padma River char area of Bagha upazila in Rajshahi....

item.description: Chief of air staff of the Bangladesh Air Force Air-Chief-Marshal Hasan Mahmood Khan has recently joined the Pacific Air Chiefs Symposium 2026 in Honolulu, Hawaii....

item.description: The High Court on Tuesday issued a rule asking the authorities concerned to explain why the decision to allocate space on streets and footpaths for hawkers should not be declared illegal....
